speaker: interviewer
text: Tape 4. Mendel Steinberg.

speaker: interviewee
text: One time, I came from work, from the lumber yard, and my wife told me, you know, the business agent from the butcher's called me and he told me he has a job for you. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] And he gave her the address with the number telephone, should I call over there. I've been told for days when night came, [INAUDIBLE] when I was working even in the lumberyard, he saw in me something. I don't know what he saw in me over there. In Cleveland, there was the [INAUDIBLE] rabbi, he called. The [INAUDIBLE] rabbi-- he was a big rabbi. He is now in [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Israel. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Even now he is living [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. He is, over there, a big rabbi. Everyone knew about him. He was, one time, in Cleveland. He had a shul in Cleveland. And he make me a [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] shul. And I was [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] shul. And this butcher that sent for me, he was the president of the shul. He didn't know-- the butcher didn't know who I am. He didn't know. Maybe he didn't took me to work, [INAUDIBLE]. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And I don't know. I came-- I came-- I heard the message, and then I came. Oh, he says, hold on. Today, in the morning, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. I came. I say [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] Zoldan, Mr. Zoldan, are you the man say-- [INAUDIBLE] told me you need a man to work. Oh, Mendel, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] He said, yeah, yeah. Now, I know it wasn't big, but he not [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH], not know-- He was knowing who I am. And anyway, at the butcher, it wasn't like should be, like should be kosher-- like, kosher-- like, should be kosher. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] No. OK. He didn't have the choice over there. And we were over there, and it was a shame. I was working by him. And no-- and [INAUDIBLE] was happy with it. He could not keep me. I didn't-- I didn't know at the time so much, but he did. Now, one time, he came, and he said, Mendel, I'm selling my meat market. I'm growing tired. That was a lie. And actually, with the butcher, what he did was a big business. And you know, in here, he cannot [INAUDIBLE]. [INAUDIBLE] things about the kosher butcher, not [INAUDIBLE]. He can't keep me. [INAUDIBLE] called the rabbi. He told him about me. And he had [INAUDIBLE] him. He said, no, it's not kosher. It's not right. [INAUDIBLE] Mendel Steinberg, when he's working by you, you have to ask him if he wants to buy. But if he doesn't want to buy, then you can sell it to whom you want. He was listening to him. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] He told me this. Then I said, I'll tell you-- I told him, Zoldan, you give me time till tomorrow morning. Tomorrow morning, I'll give you answer. Yeah, OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: Overnight, I was thinking over, and I was thinking over. What can I lose? I will buy. And I bought the butcher shop.

speaker: interviewee
text: [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] And now, when in Cleveland, the people was knowing me, who I am. Then came [INAUDIBLE] Yeshiva. He's [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. He left after the war. He see-- this was from [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH], the [? whole rabbi ?] the biggest rabbi. [INAUDIBLE] Katz was his name. He said to me [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH], till today, we didn't have, in Cleveland, such a butcher like you, or such a man. Now, if you want, we are buying meat now in New York. Now we have a such Jew like you. It's not like your shop. [INAUDIBLE] right we should buy in New York.

speaker: interviewer
text: You are more-- more believable than the other butcher. Why? Even they give me a letter. I'm not just a kosher butcher, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] the best kosher butchers. From the [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH].

speaker: interviewee
text: And I was for almost 40 years till the [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] took with me meat till I decided [INAUDIBLE]. And I had a better, better [INAUDIBLE]. I was [INAUDIBLE]. [INAUDIBLE], but he didn't hear me, [INAUDIBLE]. And I was thinking, I had all the property. I have a lot to live. I sold my meat market. My market market was bought by Jeffrey. He was a meat market-- might make a meat market from this. And I gave up the business. I gave up the business, and I had property. Then I started to buy property-- poor houses-- fix them out, sell, rent out, [INAUDIBLE] like you see in the [INAUDIBLE]. One thing to know with me-- I'm [INAUDIBLE]. See, I'm lucky, but I-- I'm lucky, but I found a real [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] real [INAUDIBLE] Jewish woman. She is so like me. She keeps kosher. She promised me, and she kept it more than I. Now I've been living here, in Fort Lauderdale, and we have a happy life together. We respect one another.

speaker: interviewee
text: And people also are talking about us. But you see, not the first wife-- the first wife was name of [INAUDIBLE]. She had a bad family. Even for shamer Shabbos, there was no family. And he told me like this-- either to be like your girlfriends-- [INAUDIBLE] then we have to go to shul to go to the picture shows. Should I go dancing?

speaker: interviewee
text: Then I gave up her. I gave up her ideas. Then I found my life. A life about my life, but I like what she likes. She was two-- she was now a widow. She had two husbands. One, she had a husband was 20 years older than she. And she had a miserable life. He died. And that is not forgot. And she said it was a miracle, too.

speaker: interviewer
text: Why is miracle? She said it like this-- one time, she was living by [INAUDIBLE]. And-- and I don't know what happened, but the whole thing-- she said he came to her. He said, Esther, did you hear what's happened? Mendel Steinberg, he's got divorced with wife. You were divorced?

speaker: interviewee
text: Getting divorced with the wife, yeah. And then she didn't know. She asked later, who's Mendel Steinberg? He's a butcher. And he's a [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. Well, she said, maybe he's good for me. [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. She said, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. And she did it. And I make sure that happened now.

speaker: interviewer
text: What is her name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Louisa-- Leah. She had-- she had a name like [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] her mother's. It was [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] Leah. She was like the mother's name. She had to keep a real Jewish name, too-- Leah.

speaker: interviewer
text: Let me ask you, Mr. Steinberg, you had another child after Hanasel?

speaker: interviewee
text: I have a daughter.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was the daughter born?

speaker: interviewee
text: What? She was born in Germany. [INAUDIBLE] She came here. She didn't [INAUDIBLE] one day in-- all the years, she was in private school. She wasn't in public school. She went [INAUDIBLE] Academy. She finished at the Academy. She went to New York, in-- in-- in-- where there [INAUDIBLE] a girls' school. And she finished over there. And she married a rabbi. The rabbi is a rabbi named Warshaw. They are living now in Georgia.

speaker: interviewer
text: How many children did she have? What is her name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Her name is Sarah. She could not have children. Then I-- I-- adopted a son, a boy. When I was adopting the son, I was-- I want to adopt such a child so you know he had a Jewish mother. And this did not happen. I was waiting, and waiting, and waiting time. My son-- my son was helped Rabbi-- by Rabbi Schauffman in New York. Rabbi [? Schauffman ?] is a known rabbi. Was-- was-- He was long years-- about 40 years-- in New York. And now he's in Israel. He was [INAUDIBLE] rabbi over there. One time, came to him one man in front of his members, and he said he had [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]-- For this-- [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: And then you adopted-- then she adopted?

speaker: interviewee
text: I adopted. [INAUDIBLE] He's now 60 years old. He's [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH].

speaker: interviewer
text: What is his name?

speaker: interviewee
text: What?

speaker: interviewer
text: What is his name?

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: And do you have grandchildren from your son?

speaker: interviewee
text: I have three grandchildren from my son.

speaker: interviewer
text: What are their names?

speaker: interviewee
text: Their name-- I had a daughter-in-law. She was no good. She was from the nicest family, but [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. Maybe it was [INAUDIBLE]. Professor [INAUDIBLE]-- he was his mother's first cousin. But she was, you know-- Her mother died young. She was raised no good. And I didn't let him take her, but he didn't listen to his father. Then [INAUDIBLE] he divorced her. And she was raised [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. That wasn't-- I was-- don't pay. I pay. And [INAUDIBLE] Academy. And after that, they can go to college, like my daughter. She did not. She was the one making the daughters for [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: So--

speaker: interviewee
text: They are now in Cleveland.

speaker: interviewer
text: What are their names?

speaker: interviewee
text: The oldest one [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] is a prolific girl. Came-- she is a genius. She was, six years, vice-president for the [INAUDIBLE]-- vice-president.

speaker: interviewer
text: And her name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Heidi Steinberg. She didn't change her name. She-- she was-- [INAUDIBLE]. She got married to a nice, Jewish boy. His name was Solomon. She didn't want to change her name. Why? She's a-- six years, you know, Heidi Steinberg, now she [INAUDIBLE] America don't know who she is. She was keeping the old name. And now, she was six years with the Solomon Brothers. And then came the Lehman Brothers. Sent an agent-- they offered her $200,000 a year. She left Solomon Brothers, for $150,000, to be with the Lehman Brothers. And she was there for two years now. She left Lehman Brothers, and she went to a self-owned business.

speaker: interviewer
text: And you have two other grandchildren. What are their names?

speaker: interviewee
text: Then the other grandchildren-- there's one whose name is Marilyn, and one is named [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: Now I'd like to ask you, Mr. Steinberg, now that's the war is over for more than 50 years, what message do you have for the world, from your experiences? What would you like to leave as a message for everybody?

speaker: interviewee
text: Say that, to everybody, I believe one message. See, the people, they was thinking they are smart. No, not smart. They was thinking they had open everything-- open eyes. No. Do you have open eyes? No, they don't see that's what they need. The main thing I would like to-- people know-- what they should know from this-- even this week we had a [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: Every week, we read from the week the [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] Should keep Shabbos. The whole world asks now why Hitler did this. And this week, in the [? [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] ?] is the answer. You can just talk with the people-- to religion people. The Bible says, who doesn't have [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH], this man doesn't have [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. Even when he's just thinking he's smart. But [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] who have [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH], that is [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH].

speaker: interviewee
text: And this [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] stated this-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] When the Jewish people-- when Aaron [INAUDIBLE], he made this calf from gold, this is the biggest sin that Jewish people did [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: Why? The same people were the-- saw the old miracles. What did God to them? He took them out from Egypt and did such miracles-- it's impossible. And the world's people say it's not true. Why is it not true? I say, I'll tell you. You look-- You look on 'Jeopardy!' Sometimes, they ask questions from-- from-- from the [INAUDIBLE]-- from the Bible, from the old Bible. And people know even Christian people know that. And Jewish people don't even know.

speaker: interviewee
text: To keep Shabbos, that is the main thing. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] God told to Moses-- Moses was begging God for everything. And God told him, OK, I'm going to do it for you. That's what you want. I'll go for myself, not [INAUDIBLE] I'll send with you. God told me he was going to send an angel to help me. Then Moses told to God, no, God, [INAUDIBLE] I want you-- you for yourself go take-- taking the-- the-- the-- doing everything, not another. And God went, listen-- and God told to Moses, I'm doing what I can. But I like you, that's why I'm going to do it.

speaker: interviewer
text: Now, one thing-- Moshe should stop wanting God to tell to Moshe-- [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. You can put in this? [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH].

speaker: interviewee
text: [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Yes. I'm going to listen to you. [NON-ENGLISH] that means this-- every time when the Jewish people is going to be in the [INAUDIBLE], does it mean like [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. God, he-- he-- he-- he-- he made himself blind. He made himself, [INAUDIBLE]. In the Jes-- in the old world, [INAUDIBLE] [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. God-- God wants the people to know, but God was warning. Moshe was obeying. And Moshe was warning the Jewish people-- about what the-- [INAUDIBLE] doing now. While in Israel, God was trying to give to them Israel. But tell Israel you're not going to be Jewish. You're not going to be keep [INAUDIBLE]. God knows what-- what in-- in-- in Israel is going to be, what will happen in Israel.

speaker: interviewer
text: Every Jew, even [INAUDIBLE] need go listen my words. I know it's going to be such rabbis, where they are wondering, you go, who's Mendel Steinberg? Well, he knows this. I say, so is. You can give my answer this for people [INAUDIBLE] people when they say no God in the world-- [INAUDIBLE]. They say, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. Your father did this. What are you doing? Your BURY your father? Your father's a [INAUDIBLE]? But you-- [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. And for this, we people-- the orthodox people-- we are suffering for the people who they are not keeping Shabbos.

speaker: interviewee
text: And now, came to me people when they are INT1erested in this. [SOBS] [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: And we was want to show the world should everyone [INAUDIBLE], should [INAUDIBLE] for this. Are you up? [INAUDIBLE] for this. That time, you can see it. Maybe I'm not going to live at that time. You young people, you're going to live. Maybe you're going--

speaker: interviewee
text: [CRYING] Remember me. My-- you was talking to such and such a man, and he told me this.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you very, very much, Mr. Steinberg for a very wonderful interview. I thank you from the bottom of my heart.

speaker: interviewer
text: Who's in this picture?

speaker: interviewer

text: Why don't you look at that, over there?

text: Who is in the picture? You tell them.

speaker: interviewee
text: Their name is Feiga, the older sister. She killed from Hitler [INAUDIBLE]. Whole And the name is Bunia. She was living in Israel 40 years. She never wanted become an American.

text: This picture is taken-- I can remember. It was supposed to be maybe 20 years. Maybe not.

speaker: interviewee
text: Dubno. They used to call [INAUDIBLE] Dubno.

speaker: interviewer

speaker: interviewee
text: It was taken in Dubno.

speaker: interviewer

speaker: interviewee
text: Me, Mendel Steinberg. Now, it's my daughter-- Susie Warshaw-- Sheindel Steinberg, my first wife.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was it taken? About when?

speaker: interviewee
text: That was taken [PAUSES FOR 7 SECONDS] about 20 years-- no, 18 years.

speaker: interviewee
text: Here, America.

speaker: interviewer

text: That was taken here, in Cleveland.
